item.title: Visualizing Medical Code Hierarchy 摘要
item.description:
This post visualizes the distribution of HCPCS and ICD-10 medical codes by their starting letter using treemaps created with Python's squarify library. The treemaps show the number of codes beginning with each letter, with square sizes proportional to code counts. For HCPCS, codes starting with G, J, L, A, and E are most common, while R and U are omitted due to small counts. For ICD-10, S, T, M, V, and H dominate, with U omitted. The code used to generate the treemaps is provided, differing only in the data dictionary for each code set.
标签: Python, squarify, treemap, HCPCS, ICD-10, medical codes, data visualization, matplotlib, healthcare, coding

item.title: Happy iCal Day 摘要
item.description:
Joanna Stern explains why the calendar emoji (📅) is correct today, July 17, which is iCal Day. The date references the original iCal announcement by Apple in 2002. The post also notes the tear-off calendar emoji (📆) is appropriate as well.
标签: emoji, calendar, iCal, Apple, technology, history, anniversary

item.title: ArtfulType: Markdown Writing App for the 68K Macintosh 摘要
item.description:
Sean Malseed created ArtfulType, a Markdown editor for the original 68K Macintosh, written in C using the Retro68 compiler. The app features a full-screen, distraction-free mode, which the author criticizes as un-Mac-like, preferring windowed interfaces like BBEdit. BBEdit, originally used for Markdown, won't run on early Macs with limited memory or older System versions. The author acknowledges the retro appeal but questions the practicality of using a Mac that can't run System 6.
标签: Markdown, Macintosh, retro computing, editor, BBEdit, 68K, Retro68, C programming, distraction-free, vintage software

item.title: BBEdit 16 摘要
item.description:
BBEdit 16 was released just before WWDC, featuring expanded Shortcuts support, text search in images, W3C HTML syntax checker, and vi keyboard emulation. New licenses cost $60, upgrades from v15 are $30, and from older versions $40. A Mac App Store subscription is available for $5/month or $50/year. The release notes are thoroughly documented.
标签: BBEdit, text editor, macOS, Shortcuts, HTML, vi emulation, software update, pricing

item.title: Linus Torvalds: ‘AI Is a Tool, Just Like Other Tools We Use. And It’s Clearly a Useful One.’ 摘要
item.description:
Linus Torvalds, the top-level maintainer of Linux, firmly states that Linux is not an anti-AI project. He acknowledges that some people dislike AI, but he is willing to put his foot down on this issue. Torvalds views AI as a tool, similar to other tools used in development, and asserts that its usefulness is no longer in question, unlike a year ago. He suggests that those who disagree can fork the project or walk away. Torvalds also notes that while there are other questions about AI, such as its economic impact, its utility is now undeniable.
标签: Linus Torvalds, Linux, AI, open source, technology, development, tools, maintainer, controversy, opinion

item.title: OpenAI Starts Cleaning Up the Utter Mess It Made of ChatGPT 摘要
item.description:
OpenAI's engineering lead Thibault Sottiaux announced updates to the new ChatGPT desktop app, including visible conversation history in the sidebar, sync across platforms, and easier switching between Chat and Work modes. However, the rollout has been heavily criticized. The new app is a 1.5 GB Electron-based monstrosity, and the update process is confusing: the old app's update button may do nothing if the new app was installed, requiring manual installation of 'ChatGPT Classic'. The situation is compared to the New Coke fiasco.
标签: OpenAI, ChatGPT, desktop app, software update, Electron, UI/UX, criticism, bloatware, sync, product management

item.title: MG Siegler: ‘OpenAI Makes ChatGPT ChatGPT Again’ 摘要
item.description:
MG Siegler criticizes OpenAI's rollout of merging Codex into ChatGPT, arguing the new app is bloated and confusing. He notes that while OpenAI quickly fixed some issues, the fundamental problem remains: renaming Codex to 'ChatGPT' and adding complexity alienates the core user base who loved ChatGPT's simplicity. The author agrees, calling the decision a 'colossal fuck-up' and suggesting OpenAI should revert to separate apps or rename Codex to 'ChatGPT Codex' instead. He blames AI researchers making product decisions without understanding user needs, comparing it to replacing Apple's Messages app with Xcode renamed 'Messages'.
标签: OpenAI, ChatGPT, Codex, product design, user experience, tech criticism, app redesign, AI products, branding, software bloat

item.title: OpenAI’s Product Shake-Up Put the Complexifiers in Charge 摘要
item.description:
OpenAI has reorganized its product teams, merging ChatGPT, Codex, and the developer API under a single core product group led by Codex head Thibault Sottiaux. The author criticizes this move, arguing that Codex is sprawling and confusing, and that putting its leaders in charge has injected ChatGPT with the same lack of focus. They contrast this with ChatGPT's earlier clarity and coherence, and criticize the trend of complex AI products like Claude, which have multiple overlapping features. The author concludes that these products only appear coherent but are actually poorly integrated.
标签: OpenAI, ChatGPT, Codex, product management, AI agents, Anthropic, Claude, software complexity, tech criticism, organizational change

item.title: The Terminus 摘要
item.description:
The article explores the anticlimactic nature of highway termini, contrasting them with the ancient Roman reverence for boundaries embodied by the god Terminus. It discusses how major U.S. highways like Interstate 64 often end unceremoniously, while Romans celebrated boundary markers with festivals and sacrifices. The piece highlights the historical and cultural significance of termini, noting that the word 'terminus' originates from a Roman god who protected borders. It also lists notable U.S. highway endpoints, such as Route 66 in Santa Monica and U.S. 1 in Key West, emphasizing how some cities celebrate their termini while others do not.
标签: highways, terminus, Roman mythology, U.S. roads, history, infrastructure, boundaries, culture, travel, geography

item.title: Apple Raises Prices for Apple Music and Apple One Subscriptions 摘要
item.description:
Apple has raised prices for Apple Music and Apple One subscriptions. Apple Music Individual increases from $10.99 to $11.99, Family from $16.99 to $19.99, and Student from $5.99 to $6.99. Apple One Individual remains at $19.95, while Family rises from $25.95 to $27.95 and Premier from $37.95 to $39.95. The changes take effect immediately for new subscribers and will apply to existing subscribers on their next billing cycle.
标签: Apple, Apple Music, Apple One, price increase, subscription, streaming, music, tech news

item.title: Roblox Set to Introduce AI Game-Building Feature, Including on iOS 摘要
item.description:
Roblox announces Build, a mobile-first creation tab, and AI-powered tools in Studio, enabling users to delegate game development tasks. The feature will be tested on July 28. The author criticizes Apple for allowing Roblox's AI-assisted game generation on iOS while banning similar AI coding apps like Bitrig, Replit, and Vibecode, suggesting Apple's inconsistent policy favors large platforms.
标签: Roblox, AI, game development, iOS, Apple, App Store, mobile creation, developer tools, policy, inconsistency

item.title: Google Runs Out of Appeals, Must Pay Record $4.7 Billion EU Antitrust Fine 摘要
item.description:
The European Court of Justice upheld a €4.1 billion ($4.67 billion) fine against Google for abusing Android's dominance to favor its own apps through pre-installation deals. Google had appealed the 2018 European Commission ruling but now has no further recourse. The fine represents about 3% of Google's $132 billion profit in 2025, but would have been 15% of its $31 billion profit in 2018. By delaying appeals as profits grew fivefold, Google effectively reduced the relative financial impact.
标签: Google, EU, antitrust, fine, Android, European Commission, European Court of Justice, competition law, tech regulation, legal
